semanticOwner: apps/agent/runbooks/** and apps/codex-proxy/runbooks/**, to be created by HGI-303
evidence: docs/README.md identifies no canonical central docs/runbooks tree and assigns HGI-303 the missing target-owned operation routes.
The canonical HGI-303 task names app-owned runbook paths and requires provider fallback readback there.
rejectedAlternatives: Create a competing central docs/runbooks tree for agent and codex-proxy procedures.
Keep consequential procedure in READMEs, skills, architecture, or dated receipts.
acceptedOrDefaultBehavior: Use one app-owned runbook tree per operational target: apps/agent/runbooks for agent, deployment, Executor, Sendblue, and incident work; apps/codex-proxy/runbooks for proxy auth and proof work. Central docs route to them and retain only durable authority rationale.

taskBlocking.inheritedNonClaim: No app operation is owned until target-owned runbooks exist with authority, rollback, and escalation.

authorityImpact: Ownership routing grants no provider authority; every consequential step remains approval-gated by its app-owned runbook and external target.
nonClaims: No runbook, provider procedure, or external recovery path is implemented by this record.
Central documentation is not declared an operational owner.
carryingCost: Maintain pointers and review each app runbook when its app, provider, authentication, deployment, or incident boundary changes.
reviewOrResumeTrigger: Review or supersede when HGI-303 changes the accepted app-owned runbook inventory or a new operational target appears.
